Chanel: The classic. A timeless abstract floral. In 1921, Gabrielle Chanel asked Ernest Beaux to create a woman's perfume with the scent of a woman. A visionary composition. It is a mythical fragrance, an emblem of Parisian elegance that has passed through generations.
Hermes: Feel the earth. Terre d'Hermès tells the story of the mythical elements of the natural world. A journey through the wood and the stone. It is a quiet strength, a poetic translation of the raw materials that surround us. An invitation to explore the world's origins.
YSL: Do you hear the call? Black Opium is the seduction of the night. A shot of adrenaline, a whispered secret in the dark. Follow your desires into the neon lights. It draws you in close, wrapping you in an addictive, intimate embrace.
Maison Margiela: Replica. Reproduction of familiar scents and moments of varying locations and periods. Each scent is tied to a specific memory. It is the exact smell of a lazy Sunday morning in Florence, 2003. A delicate, hyper-personal vignette of a time you can never return to.
Jean Paul Gaultier: The terrible child of fashion. Le Mâle returns to the port. He is the classic sailor, a cheeky wink to traditional masculinity. Step right up to the show. A playful, seductive character known by everyone in the room.
Viktor & Rolf: We want to spread a positive message. Flowerbomb is our way of making the world a bit more magical. An explosion of petals designed to transform the negative into the positive. Welcome to our surreal, floral universe.
Mancera: A journey to the edge of the Orient. Mancera pairs Western design with the deepest, richest ouds and spices of the East. A luxurious expedition into the world of rare materials. Crafted for those who appreciate the deep traditions of perfumery.
Creed: From father to son since 1760. The House of Creed relies on a 4,000-year-old infusion technique. We are a family of artisans crafting scents by hand. Our legacy is built on the quiet dedication to the finest ingredients.
Byredo: I founded Byredo because I wanted to translate my own memories into smells. Mojave Ghost is about survival. It's a deeply personal reflection on my travels. The scents are just extensions of my diary, naked and completely transparent.
